turns.user_message: I tried doing all those little things people say actually work, like taking a long walk and listening to upbeat music, but nothing really stuck. It’s only been a few days since this heavy feeling settled in, and I keep waiting for it to just lift on its own. I honestly thought I could just shake it off by keeping busy, but every little setback feels way bigger than it should be. I’m starting to wonder if I’m just fundamentally broken in some way that I can’t fix.

I keep trying to distract myself with new routines and positive lists, but the same hollow feeling just creeps back in every single evening. What started as a rough patch has turned into this exhausting loop where I constantly measure myself against some invisible standard I can never reach. I catch myself apologizing for existing in shared spaces and assuming my presence is just an inconvenience to everyone else. It feels like I’m slowly losing whatever confidence I used to carry around so easily.

I’ve completely stopped trying to force myself into a better headspace because every single effort just proves how deeply flawed I really am underneath. This isn’t just a passing mood anymore, it’s a constant heavy weight that makes me question every single choice I make and every word I say. I feel completely hollowed out and convinced that I’m just taking up space that someone more capable should have instead. It’s exhausting to pretend I’m fine when I know I’m fundamentally falling short of what anyone actually deserves.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This just started recently.
